intent: process the first sample with scanpy

blocks.text: I'll process the first sample (Patient 145, Day 0) with scanpy using the standard QC → normalization → HVG → PCA → clustering → UMAP workflow.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "body": "scRNA-seq QC+clustering (scanpy): read 10x; calculate_qc_metrics; filter n_genes>=200 & pct_mito<0.20; normalize_total(1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; pca; neighbors; umap; leiden; rank_genes_groups. Plots: HVG plot and PCA variance side-by-side; UMAP colored by leiden; use sc.pl.* not hand-rolled matplotlib."}

blocks.text: Now let me present the plan:

input.title: Process first COVID-19 PBMC sample (GSM5746259) with scanpy QC + clustering
input.rationale: Standard single-cell RNA-seq pipeline to assess cell quality, identify major cell populations, and generate visualizations.

steps.title: Load the first sample (Patient 145, Day 0)
steps.description: Extract GSM5746259 from the combined dataset; 7,532 cells × 36,601 genes

steps.title: Calculate QC metrics
steps.description: Compute n_genes, n_counts, pct_counts_mito for each cell using Ensembl/NCBI mitochondrial gene list

steps.title: Filter low-quality cells
steps.description: Remove cells with n_genes < 200 or pct_counts_mito ≥ 20%

steps.title: Identify highly variable genes
steps.description: Scanpy highly_variable_genes with default settings; plot HVG + PCA variance

steps.title: Neighborhood & clustering
steps.description: Compute neighbors, run UMAP, Leiden clustering (resolution 1.0); plot UMAP colored by cluster

steps.title: Marker genes
steps.description: rank_genes_groups per cluster; plot top genes

assumptions: Using the combined h5ad saved from GEO (GSE192391_first_two_samples_combined.h5ad)
Mitochondrial genes are those with names starting with 'MT-' (HGNC convention for human)
PCA: use all HVGs, default n_pcs=50
Leiden clustering: resolution 1.0 (moderate granularity for PBMC identification)
